title: Area of Circle
text: What is the area of a circle with radius 7 cm? (Use π = 22/7)
explanation: Area = πr² = (22/7) × 7² = (22/7) × 49 = 22 × 7 = 154 cm². Choosing 44 cm² is the circumference (2πr), not area. Choosing 308 cm² uses diameter instead of radius: π(14)² = 616/2 = 308. Choosing 77 cm² uses πr instead of πr².

title: Triangle Angle Sum
text: Two angles of a triangle are 45° and 75°. What is the third angle?
explanation: Sum of angles in a triangle = 180°. Third angle = 180 - 45 - 75 = 60°. Choosing 90° adds the given angles (45+75=120) and subtracts from 180 incorrectly: 180-120=60 - wait that gives 60 too. Let me recalculate: 180 - 45 - 75 = 60. So 90 is wrong because it would make the sum 45+75+90=210, exceeding 180. Choosing 30° incorrectly assumes the angles are complementary.

title: Pythagorean Triple
text: Which of the following sets of side lengths forms a right-angled triangle?

title: Volume of Cylinder
text: What is the volume of a cylinder with radius 3 cm and height 10 cm? (Use π = 3.14)
explanation: Volume = πr²h = 3.14 × 9 × 10 = 282.6 cm³. Choosing 94.2 cm³ uses only πr² (area of base, ignoring height). Choosing 188.4 cm³ uses the wrong radius value. Choosing 565.2 cm³ doubles the result or uses diameter instead of radius.

title: Parallel Lines
text: If two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, which pair of angles are always equal?
explanation: When parallel lines are cut by a transversal, corresponding angles are equal. Adjacent angles are supplementary (sum to 180°), not necessarily equal. Complementary angles sum to 90° and are not a standard pair in this context.

title: Area of Triangle
text: A triangle has base 12 cm and height 8 cm. What is its area?
explanation: Area = (1/2) × base × height = (1/2) × 12 × 8 = 48 cm². Choosing 96 cm² forgets to divide by 2. Choosing 24 cm² multiplies base by height/4 or uses wrong dimensions.

title: Perimeter of Rectangle
text: A rectangle has length 15 cm and width 10 cm. What is its perimeter?
explanation: Perimeter = 2(l + w) = 2(15 + 10) = 2 × 25 = 50 cm. Choosing 150 cm is the area (15 × 10). Choosing 25 cm forgets to multiply by 2. Choosing 100 cm uses 4 times the sum instead of 2 times.

title: Angle in a Semicircle
text: An angle inscribed in a semicircle is always:
explanation: The angle inscribed in a semicircle (Thales' theorem) is always a right angle (90°). This is a fundamental theorem in geometry.

title: Surface Area of Cube
text: What is the total surface area of a cube with side 5 cm?
explanation: Surface area = 6 × side² = 6 × 25 = 150 cm². Choosing 125 cm² is the volume (5³). Choosing 100 cm² uses 4 sides instead of 6.

title: Similar Triangles
text: Two similar triangles have areas in ratio 4 : 9. What is the ratio of their corresponding sides?
explanation: For similar triangles, areas are proportional to the square of corresponding sides. So side ratio = √(area ratio) = √(4/9) = 2/3 = 2 : 3. Choosing 4 : 9 uses the area ratio directly without taking the square root.
